title: Geopolitics and International Security: Research & Analysis | CSIS
text:
Geopolitics and International Security

Prev

Global Governance and Institutions

Allies and Partners

Next

CSIS experts utilize deep expertise and global relationships to help policymakers in navigate the evolving challenges to U.S. global leadership. Their work examines the key political, economic, security, and societal trends shaping the future of global governance and institutions.

Image

Photo: Jaap Arriens/NurPhoto/Getty Images

Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Terrorism

AI will not transform terrorism overnight. Its more likely effect is subtler: lowering barriers for extremist propaganda, recruitment, disinformation, and planning while strengthening counterterrorism tools and surveillance risks.

Russian Roulette

Ukraine's Dynamic Defense Sector: Combat Hubs, Air Defense, and Non-Stop Innovation

Investigative journalist Tim Mak, who has been stationed in Ukraine since 2022, joins Max and Maria for a discussion about life in Kyiv, the processes and challenges driving innovation in Ukraine’s dynamic civilian defense combat hubs, and the impact of AI and information integration on the battlefield.

The Eurofile LIVE: German Rearmament and Reflections on NATO's Ankara Summit

Event

— July 9, 2026

Pekingology

Avoiding War in the South China Sea

Henrietta Levin is joined by Greg Poling, Director and Senior Fellow of the CSIS Asia Maritime Transparency Initiative and Southeast Asia Program, for a wide-ranging discussion on China’s strategy, successes, and failures in the South China Sea.

and

Formula 1 on the Front Lines

Hosting F1 races was supposed to showcase the Gulf's post-oil future. Instead, the Iran war canceled two Grand Prix races and two more are under threat. What does this mean for the future of hosting major global events in contested regions?

Commentary
by

Emily Harding
and
Cynthia R. Cook

— July 9, 2026

The Geography of Coercion: Russian Missile and Drone Campaigns in Ukraine

How does Russia decide where to strike? This analysis maps the evolving geography of Russian missile and drone attacks, revealing how Moscow targets Ukraine’s military, infrastructure, and cities to maximize coercive pressure.

by

Marcus Welsch,

and
Erik Tiersten-Nyman

— July 7, 2026

What Does NATO Defense Spending Look Like Heading into the Ankara Summit?

As NATO allies meet in Ankara this week, defense spending will likely be on the agenda. Seamus Daniels outlines recent trends as NATO commits to increased spending.

Critical Questions
by
Seamus P. Daniels

— July 6, 2026
